# codex-acp v0.23.5

This release is the first release prepared around the fork's own local adapter
contract instead of older compatibility paths. The biggest practical change is
the move to adapter-owned configuration under `~/.codex-cas/`, with editable
JSONC files generated by the adapter itself.

## Highlights

- Adapter-owned config and cache now live under `~/.codex-cas/` by default.
- `selector-preferences.json` controls visible selectors, grouped model entries,
  default model/reasoning/speed values, layout order, and slash command
  visibility.
- `display-maps.json` controls compact context and limit labels. Hidden runtime
  percent/braille display fallbacks were removed, so selected display maps must
  exist and be valid.
- Existing invalid JSONC config now fails startup with the file path instead of
  being overwritten or silently ignored.
- A new `install.sh` can install from source, install an existing downloaded
  binary, or download the matching GitHub Release artifact for the current
  Linux/macOS host.
- Local release handling is stricter: the helper no longer keeps rollback binary
  copies, and release preparation builds before creating the tag.

## Install

On Linux/macOS:

```bash
curl -fsSLO https://github.com/Qweasd123tg/zed-codex-ACP-CAS/releases/latest/download/install.sh
bash install.sh
```

For a pinned release:

```bash
bash install.sh --download 0.23.5
```

The installer puts the binary at `~/.local/bin/codex-acp`, creates
`~/.codex-cas/`, smoke-tests the installed binary, writes
`codex-acp.build-info.txt`, and prints a ready-to-paste Zed settings snippet.

## Zed Configuration

Add the installed adapter as a custom external agent:

```json
{
  "agent_servers": {
    "codex-acp-cas": {
      "type": "custom",
      "command": "/home/your-user/.local/bin/codex-acp"
    }
  }
}
```

The adapter starts `codex app-server` internally. If Zed does not inherit a
`PATH` that can find `codex`, set `CODEX_ACP_CODEX_BIN` to the absolute backend
binary path in the environment that launches Zed.

## Notable Fixes And Cleanup

- Removed legacy CAS migration from old `$CODEX_HOME/codex-acp/` and
  `$CODEX_HOME/memories/codex-acp/` locations.
- Removed old config-option aliases and slash-command compatibility aliases that
  were no longer part of the current UX contract.
- Made startup timeout overrides strict. Invalid, zero, or non-Unicode
  `CODEX_ACP_STARTUP_TIMEOUT_MS` values now produce an explicit error instead
  of silently falling back.
- Tightened `/archive` and `/unarchive` parsing so prefix strings like
  `/archived` are not treated as commands.
- Simplified local release builds to one current binary at
  `.build/codex-acp-current` plus build-info metadata.

## Platform Notes

- Linux `x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu` is the primary tested target.
- macOS Apple Silicon and Windows x64 artifacts are built by the release
  workflow, but Windows still benefits from a wrapper that explicitly sets
  `CODEX_ACP_CODEX_BIN` and, if needed, `CODEX_CAS_HOME`.
